original_abstract:
To ensure a targeted retrieval of pertinent academic literature, we systematically screened the Dimensions AI database, specifically targeting the "Title" and "Abstract" fields. Our search strategy utilized key terms such as "sex factor*", "gender difference*", and "sex specific*", while explicitly excluding terms related to sexual behavior or selection to maintain thematic focus. From an initial pool of 5,103,170 articles and 4,410,763 grants, we identified 324,639 studies and 21,250 grants that directly addressed sex and gender characteristics within biomedical and health-related disciplines—including clinical sciences, psychology, and public health—where such considerations are paramount.

The inclusion criteria were restricted to research employing sex/gender-disaggregated data or investigating sex/gender-based health outcomes. We covered the period from 2000 to 2021 to effectively capture shifts in research trends before and after the implementation of major international sex/gender-specific funding and editorial policies. Data retrieval was finalized on March 3, 2023. To quantify the integration of these characteristics, we calculated the percentage of papers and grants containing relevant keywords relative to the total volume of output. Statistical analyses were performed using GraphPad Prism 10 to evaluate the longitudinal impact of institutional policy changes on the representation of sex and gender in the scientific record.
humanized_abstract:
We implemented targeted search strategies to maximize the yield of relevant literature. In the Dimensions AI database, we searched in the “Title” and “Abstract” fields using the following search terms: “sex factor*”, “gender difference*”, and “sex specific*”. These terms were combined with a sexual behavior/selection exclusion filter to ensure relevance to the core objective of this study. From 5,103,170 articles and 4,410,763 grants, we filtered the data to include only those with direct relevance to sex and gender biological characteristics within the scope of biomedical and health-related fields, which primarily consist of clinical sciences, psychology, and public health. Our search ultimately yielded 324,639 papers and 21,250 grants.

Studies were selected based on strict inclusion criteria that defined only research with sex/gender-disaggregated data or sex/gender-specific health outcomes. Our review examines publications and funding over the period 2000-2021, before and after implementation of major new editorial and funding policies with a focus on sex/gender. Data for this analysis were pulled on March 3, 2023. To estimate the extent to which sex and gender are being integrated into various scientific fields, we calculated the percentage of papers and grants that include the sex and gender keywords compared with the total number of papers and grants. Analyses were performed using GraphPad Prism 10 software to determine the effects over time of changes in institutional policies on the numbers of papers that report on the sex and gender of individuals included in studies.
